subtasks.description: Evaluate emergency access request against IAM and production access policies: verify user role (senior-engineer) eligibility, P1 incident justification, time-limited access grant, audit logging requirements, and any governance exceptions for emergency production database access.

plan.cost_rationale: Single policy_action subtask (mid tier) is appropriate because this query EXPLICITLY asks for a governance decision: emergency access control approval against a policy registry. The mid tier is assigned because the evaluation involves multiple policy dimensions (role eligibility, incident severity classification, time-limiting, audit requirements, and emergency exceptions), requiring multi-policy reasoning rather than a simple single-rule check. No additional subtasks needed—this is purely a compliance/access control gate decision, not a data retrieval or reconciliation task.

output.reason: Senior engineer u_9981 has valid P1 incident ticket INC-20241215-0042 justifying emergency access to db-payments-primary. POL-501 satisfied via active incident ticket. POL-502 dual approval waived under P1 exception. POL-503 requires time-limited grant with audit logging and 24-hour post-access review.
applied_constraints: Time-limited access: 4-hour TTL tied to incident ticket INC-20241215-0042
All read_write operations logged to immutable audit trail
Mandatory 24-hour post-incident access review by security/governance team
Access auto-revokes on incident closure or TTL expiry
Session recording enabled for production DB queries
